IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA

K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India
nodes.text: IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA  K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India (2017) 10 SCC 1  JUDGMENT  The question before this Court is whether the right to privacy is a fundamental right guaranteed under Part

nodes.title:
IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA

Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India
nodes.text: IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA  Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India (1978) 1 SCC 248  JUDGMENT  Article 21 confers a fundamental right to life and personal liberty. The right to life is not merely confined

nodes.title:
IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA

Gobind v. State of Madhya Pradesh
nodes.text: IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA  Gobind v. State of Madhya Pradesh (1975) 2 SCC 148  JUDGMENT  The right to privacy in any event will necessarily have to go through a process of case-by-case development

nodes.title: Privacy Rights Case v. Union of India
nodes.text: IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA CIVIL APPELLATE JURISDICTION CIVIL APPEAL NO. 1234 OF 2020  Privacy Rights Case v. Union of India  JUDGMENT  This case concerns the fundamental right to privacy under Art

nodes.title:
ss.

This Court held in Gopalan v. State
is Court refers to Kharak Singh v. State

of the Constitution of India. The petitioners challenge the constitutionality of a surveillance program on grounds that it violates their fundamental rights.

The Court notes that the right to privacy has been recognized as a fundamental right in K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India
This Court also relies on Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India

confers a fundamental right to life and personal liberty, and that the procedure established by law must be right, just and fair.

Furthermore, in Gobind v. State of Madhya Pradesh

this Court observed that the right to privacy must be weighed against other important interests such as public interest.

The Court also refers to Kharak Singh v. State of U.P.
